errors: ["The 'ProcedureLookBack' function uses a lookback interval of 1 month for colonoscopy screening, which is not clinically reasonable. Colonoscopy screening guidelines typically recommend intervals of 10 years for average-risk patients. The lookback period should be extended to align with appropriate screening intervals (e.g., 10 years) to avoid premature or unnecessary recommendations."]

[The look-back period for procedures is set to 1 month, which is too short for standard prostate cancer screening intervals (typically annual). Additionally, the recommendation includes "biopsy" as an initial screening method, which is not clinically appropriate—biopsy is a follow-up to abnormal PSA/DRE results, not an initial screening test.]

["The HbA1c threshold of 9% (g%) in the 'HbA1c Laboratory Test' expression is clinically inconsistent with standard diagnostic criteria. Per ADA guidelines, a diagnostic threshold of ≥6.5% is recommended for Type 2 Diabetes diagnosis. The current value may incorrectly delay diagnosis or misclassify patients."]

[Minimum age for screening is set to 60 years, which conflicts with clinical guidelines (e.g., USPSTF recommends starting at 45 years for average-risk individuals). The "Has Recent Colorectal Screening" logic uses a 2-year interval, which may not align with modality-specific guidelines (e.g., colonoscopy every 10 years vs. stool-based tests every 1-2 years). This could lead to premature recommendations for patients who had longer-interval screenings.]

["In Demographic" expression uses an age threshold of 30 years, which is not aligned with standard clinical guidelines (e.g., CDC recommends HIV screening for all adults aged 13-64, regardless of age). This may exclude younger adults who should be screened.]

["In Demographic" expression checks age >=30 and <=65, but USPSTF recommends screening for women aged 21-65. The age range in the ELM (30-65) conflicts with the rationale text (21-65) and established USPSTF guidelines.]

["Has Recent Screening" combines Adult and Adolescent depression screenings without differentiating based on patient age. For patients aged 12+, Adolescent Depression Screening (PHQ-A) is inappropriate; they should only be considered for Adult Depression Screening (PHQ-9). The logic should conditionally check for the appropriate screening type based on age (e.g., PHQ-9 for ≥18, PHQ-A for 12-17 if applicable)."]

["In Demographic" expression uses age threshold of 75 years instead of the USPSTF-recommended 65 years for falls prevention screening eligibility. The rationale references 65 years but the actual logic uses 75, creating a clinical guideline implementation discrepancy.]

["In Demographic" condition incorrectly uses age >=25 instead of >=18 as stated in the rationale, conflicting with USPSTF's recommendation for screening adults 18 years or older. This creates a clinical validity mismatch between the demographic criteria and the referenced guideline.]

["Has Recent DEXA Scan" interval of 6 months conflicts with USPSTF guidance recommending retesting every 10-15 years for normal results. The 6-month window may inappropriately suppress recommendations for patients due for routine screening.]

["In Demographic" checks age 55-75 years, but USPSTF recommends 65-75 years for AAA screening. The lower age threshold (55 vs 65) is clinically incorrect. Rationale text correctly references 65-75 but logic implementation is inconsistent.]

["Needs Tobacco Use Screening" expression incorrectly references only "In Demographic" without checking for absence of recent screening (missing NOT Has Recent Tobacco Screening condition). This would recommend screening for all adults ≥18 regardless of prior screening, violating USPSTF guidelines requiring annual screening but not redundant screening within the same year.]

[1. The "In Demographic" criteria only checks age ≥50 but lacks an upper age limit (≤80) as required by USPSTF guidelines. 2. The "Has Smoking History" criteria does not quantify the 20 pack-year smoking history requirement, only checking for active tobacco use status without duration/quantity validation.]

["Needs Falls Prevention Screening" incorrectly combines "In Demographic" AND "Has Recent Falls Assessment". Clinically, the recommendation should trigger when a patient is in the demographic (age ≥65) AND does NOT have a recent falls assessment (i.e., no valid assessment in the past year). The current logic would recommend screening only if a recent assessment exists, which contradicts clinical intent. The "AND" should be replaced with "NOT Has Recent Falls Assessment".]
